title: Sovol SV08 Z offset
author: Treathers
body:
Hello, 

I currently am using eddy BTT probe with my mainline klipper SV08. My z offset is recalculated after every home, its always +.05 give or take +-.005. The issue is that's not accurate. Sometimes I have to make correction all the way to -.2 or +.2 or its perfect at .05. This happens weather its fresh power cycle or a new print.

Current start is preheat bed to 60 wait roughly 10-20 min, then my start print is home --> QGL --> home --> bed mesh --> print starts.

Not sure where to look, this is driving me insane. Had a very similar issue with stock probe and was hoping btt eddy would fix it

Thanks for any help

title: PLR customization to Klipper causes move queue overflow error, shutdown, and print failure on small moves like organic supports and spiral z-hop
author: evelant
body:
This is a critical bug in the stock firmware. Using spiral z-hop or printing some models with round shapes or organic supports is impossible as it will cause the SV08 to shutdown with "error 14 move queue overflow".

For anybody else who gets stuck with this error I found the root cause and figured out how to fix it. The problem is Sovol’s PLR (power loss recovery) customization to Klipper. This is why moving to mainline Klipper fixes it, because that removes Sovol’s PLR customization. It’s pretty easy to fix on the Sovol firmware now that we know what’s causing it.

The problem is in ~/klipper/klippy/extras/gcode_move.py [Lines 123-133](https://github.com/Sovol3d/SV08/blob/a60644875f8c756d20b3828c9416518b414b5491/home/sovol/klipper/klippy/extras/gcode_move.py#L123-L133). These lines write information to a file on the sd card synchronously on every single move command. That’s too slow, especially for python on a relatively constrained host. It causes moves to be processed too slowly when there’s a lot of short moves, the host fails to communicate with the mcu fast enough resulting in the move queue overflow error.

To fix it, ssh into the printer and simply comment out these lines in ~/klipper/klippy/extras/gcode_move.py

        if 'G' in params and 'Z' in params and 'X' in params and 'Y' in params and 'F' in params:
            if self.v_sd.cmd_from_sd:
                #commandline = gcmd.get_commandline()
                content = {
                    'commandline': gcmd.get_commandline(),
                    'Z': params['Z'],
                    'extrude_type': 'M82' if self.absolute_extrude else 'M83',
                    'e_extrude_abs': 0 #self.Coord(*self.move_position)[3]
                }
                with open("/home/sovol/sovol_plr_height", 'w') as height:
                    json.dump(content, height)
PLR will obviously no longer work, but it never worked well to begin with and failed every single time when the move queue overflow happened since it was the writing of plr info causing the failure. Hopefully Sovol fixes this in an official firmware update. It was hard to track down but in hindsight it’s a pretty obvious huge flaw in the PLR design – there’s no way that writing to a file synchronously with every move command is going to work reliably.

Now I can print as fast as I want with spiral z-hop and organic supports, no more problems!

title: Thermal runaway event when saving Z-offset
author: sanchosk
body:
I have stock SV08.
I had to replace hotend for new one due to failed print ruining my hotend.
After installing the new hotend and calibrating, everything worked fine.
I noticed that during first layer print, the surface is not as "squished" as I am used to - so I adjusted the Z-offset by 0.1mm down.
The result was excellent after 3 prints, so I decided to save the offset.
As I've hit save, the printer should reboot.
Instead, I received notice that there was MCU thermal error - reporting temperature above threshold.
I spent some 3-4 minutes trying to reboot the mainboard remotely (the printer is in my garage), no avail, constant complain about overtemperature.
So I went to garage - to find out my hotend literally smoking, half of the garage filled with nasty smoke and stink and the heater constantly on on the hotend.
I quickly plugged the printer out of the wall.
After 3-4 minutes (ambient temperature below 10 degrees in garage) the hotend was still too hot to boot, another 2-3 minutes resulted in temperature above 380 degrees and falling slowly down.
This seems to be quite critical issue - reboot causes the hotend to get stuck in full heat mode and if the printer was not completely cold (e.g. the Z-offset was saved shortly after pre-heat), the hotend will not have enough time to restart before the temperature runs out of range - at which point it will get stuck and the heater will remain on (!!!).
This is a serious issue, needs urgent attention.
In such cases, when thermal runaway is detected, the printer MUST turn the heater off before refusing to continue operation!

title: Using a Raspberry Pi as a central MCU
author: sabrinaacardoso
body: I'm using a Sovol SV08 and I'd like to know if it's possible to perform USB communication using the printer board's USB-C to UART port to migrate the klipper processing to a Rasperry Pi 5.

title: Sovol SV08: Please update outdated Klipper fork and optimize Macro.cfg and printer.cfg
author: kampfwuffi
body:
I have a notification in Mainsail since buying the Sovol SV08 running the latest firmware which is a Klipper fork by Sovol:

```
Dependency: Moonraker
The current Moonraker version does not support all features of Mainsail. Update Moonraker to at least v0.8.0-306.
```

Will there be an update to Klipper and Moonraker, since the current firmware is outdated and some features not supported (e.g. Total Print Time (hours) so history not supported in Moonraker, using "while" command and other newer Klipper functions in gcode, and other features.

I could attempt to update Moonraker by cloning the official Moonraker repository [(https://github.com/Arksine/moonraker)](https://github.com/Arksine/moonraker) and installing v0.8.0-306 manually via SSH (username/password: sovol/sovol) 
However, this is not recommended because Sovol’s customized Klipper may have dependencies incompatible with the mainline Moonraker version.
Users have reported failures when updating Moonraker due to missing or mismatched dependencies i don't want to run into.

There’s a risk of Moonraker failing to connect to Klipper, rendering the printer unusable.

Macro.cfg and printer.cfg is very basic imho too. Attached are my optimizations to make the SV08 a much better machine, maybe the Sovol Team can look into the code sections and implement some tweaks from my files to avoid some headache (like X/Y axis banging into the gantry on homing, missing preheating, filament-sensor fix which will detect runouts not only once, a better cleaning routine removing the blob from bed automaticly (need removal of blob in Machine g-code in orca too), better z-offset gcode, error handling, status colors in LCD and many fixes....

[V18-sv08-optimized-config-files.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20692615/V18-sv08-optimized-config-files.zip)

title: SECURITY: SSH host keys not generated on first boot
author: cscashby
body:
The default image has an SSH host key set up.

This host key is NOT replaced on first boot - which could be a security risk. Please add a run once startup script which includes the following:

rm /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*
ssh-keygen -A

Not doing so could leave your users open to MITM attacks

author: marcoaccasarin
body:

title: Really? What a joke of a company...
author: SoLongSidekick
body:
I've been printing since 2012, I'm not exactly a newbie when it comes to printing. I've struggled ever since I got my SV08 to print a single fucking thing. And now I come back to get a new copy of printer.cfg because I wanted to make sure I removed some recent edits to mine AND THE FREAKING DOCUMENTS ARE WRONG. Really? My printer.cfg has lines going down to line 448 and there should be an M140 line below that, then the forbidden section. The one in this githiub ends at 444. 

This printer and Sovol have been such a pathetic joke it would be funny, if I hadn't blown so much money on it. Would anyone mind posting their printer.cfg from line #439 down to the forbidden section?

title: Constant Network Flapping
author: mplinuxgeek
body:
My SV08's network interface is constantly going down/up, its done it since I got the machine (11th of October), I've tried many things to resolve it:

1. Replaced ethernet cable
2. Different switch
3. Set port speed to 100Mbps Half Duplex
4. Tried a fresh firmware image
6. Tried USB network adapters (USB Network kernel modules aren't included with the image and due to /boot being fat32 you can't install the correct packages)

Unfortunately wifi is too slow on this board to make the camera stream usable, I only get 1-2 fps over wifi yet its in the same room as an AX1800 access point.

```
sovol@SPI-XI:~$ dmesg | grep "Link is Down" | wc -l
227
sovol@SPI-XI:~$ uptime
 12:28:05 up 2 days,  5:58,  2 users,  load average: 0.05, 0.15, 0.16
```

Output from dmesg:
[dmesg.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17460742/dmesg.txt)

title: Contribution: a bayonett adapter for the spoolholder to attach a retracting strap
author: mindforger
body:
[spool holder assembly_Bayonettadapter v2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17002634/spool.holder.assembly_Bayonettadapter.v2.zip)

i made an replacement for the bayonett ring just a simple arm that fits into the spool holder with a distance ring to make it easier printable without having to support the offset of the notches when printing

you can print it diagonally on the printer without issues

the dtsance ring pushes the spool roll 2.5mm further away, so the groove for the bayonett is flush with the spool holder material to engage with the notches in the arm, twist the roll into the arm to lock and attach a retracting strap (like those key or card holders) to the end and hook the wires and bowden tube to it
![photo_5415892699162205265_y](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5f5e9175-7223-4da2-b510-ca60e06787a7)
![photo_5415892699162205263_y](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c417b58c-83cd-4f9e-9e7f-9b716931a83f)


PS: i have no idea where i could publish stuff like that, if anyone has a suggestion please tell me :D

title: Better Sensorless Homing Configuration
author: tommyboylab
body:
I'm linking a PR I have prepared to deal with an issue I found with sensor-less homing.

After turning on my printer today, It was unable to home properly and moved itself into the front-left corner of my printer before skipping a huge amount of steps and driving the nozzle into the printer bed. 

I saw on [Gergo Print 3D's video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xt6uzXgRJdc) there was an option to tune the sensitivity of the sensor-less homing. I tried this after re-positioning the printhead to the far right corner but unfortunately after the initial homing it achieved the same result (sending itself back to the front left corner to do more damage)

I then went to Klipper documentation and found the related setting listed for the drivers. Underneath it it has a section for CoreXY which states `When using sensorless homing on CoreXY, make sure there is no hold_current configured for either stepper.` on which the SV08 has configured a value of `1.5`.

 [Here's the documentation](https://www.klipper3d.org/TMC_Drivers.html#tips-for-sensorless-homing-on-corexy)

My question is, why is this present? If the Klipper documentation states it should not be for use with sensor-less homing and the documentation regarding hold_current states [it is preferred not to set it](https://www.klipper3d.org/TMC_Drivers.html#prefer-to-not-specify-a-hold_current)?

I'll leave this open for discussion if someone else may have an opinion, I am fairly new to Klipper and so do not yet have an understanding of why most options are set as they are.

Link to PR: https://github.com/Sovol3d/SV08/pull/12
